episode.title: The 3,300-Year-Old Ship Full of Royal Gold

synopsis: On the steep slope off Uluburun near Kaş, a team works the oldest shipwreck ever excavated — Bronze Age copper ingots stacked like grave rows and, hidden in the silt, a solid gold scarab bearing Nefertiti's name.
narration: In 1982, a Turkish sponge diver named Mehmet Çakır surfaced near Kaş with a strange report: metal biscuits with ears, stacked on the seabed. Archaeologists went down — and found the oldest shipwreck ever excavated. A royal trading vessel from thirty-three centuries ago, fifty meters down. Ten tons of copper. Raw blue glass. Ebony and ivory. And buried in the silt, a single scarab of solid gold, carved with the name of Queen Nefertiti. It took more than twenty-two thousand dives to bring the cargo home. The sea kept it for three thousand years. Bodrum keeps it now.

shots.prompt: POV descent along a steep underwater rock slope plunging into deep blue darkness, headlamp beam raking the incline, bubbles streaming up past the lens, a teammate diver below as a silhouette with a torch. Ends on: rows of strange rectangular slabs appearing on the slope out of the gloom.

Moving along the rows: four-handled oxhide-shaped copper ingots crusted with growth, stacked and scattered down the slope like grave markers, large clay storage jars leaning between them, torch light gliding across it all. Ends on: blackened ancient hull timbers curving up from the seabed ahead like a ribcage.

Passing between the blackened hull timbers in the torch light; a moray eel slides suddenly out of a jar mouth and vanishes into the dark, silt pulsing in the beam. Ends on: the beam falling on a patch of pale undisturbed silt between two timbers.

Close on the pale silt as gloved fingers fan it gently away: a small golden scarab emerges, impossibly bright and untouched, gleaming in the torch beam against the grey sediment, bubbles drifting up. Ends on: the gold scarab resting in a gloved palm, glinting under the light.

The gloved hands seal the scarab into a small clear sample case clipped to a lift line; the camera follows the line upward as the rows of ingots dim one by one below, the wreck sinking back into deep blue darkness while faint sunlight shimmers far overhead. Ends on: the site swallowed by depth as the surface glow strengthens.
